bounded_ingest_cap_mb.rule: start 100MB or full-if-smaller; expand to 300MB only if high-value and subgroup coverage weak; never unbounded

sources.name: mooncake
sources.main_question: Does cache_reuse_pct remain shadow-ready beyond SwissAI?
sources.paper: Mooncake: A KVCache-centric Disaggregated Architecture for LLM Serving (FAST'25; arXiv 2407.00079)
sources.exact_schema: JSONL rows: {timestamp:int(ms), input_length:int(tokens), output_length:int(tokens), hash_ids:[int...]}. block_size=512; identical hash_ids => shared prefix/KVCache reuse.
sources.license: Repo root has no standalone data LICENSE file (HTTP 404); README requests citation. Trace values are author-anonymized integers (privacy-remapped block hashes, token counts, relative timestamps) — NO raw prompt/response text exists. Commit policy: only derived NUMERIC normalized sample (no full hash_ids list), with attribution + citation.
sources.measured_vs_derived: timestamps/token-lengths/block-hash-structure are real (production-derived, anonymized); cache reuse is NOT a measured label — it must be DERIVED from hash_ids overlap (a global-prefix-cache hit SIMULATION). No model_id, no session_id, no request_id, no measured latency/queue/cache-hit.

downloaded.note: all 4 files < 5MB; full ingest under 100MB cap, no sampling needed

sources.label_compatibility_with_swissai: LIMITED — SwissAI provides MEASURED reuse_percentage (bucket overlap %); Mooncake provides only a DERIVED prefix-reuse proxy. The native targets are NOT identical; only a derived high_reuse proxy on a shared minimal feature set is cross-comparable.

sources.name: huawei_faas_2025
sources.main_question: Can public FaaS cold-start breakdowns improve simulator priors without pretending they are GPU model-load truth?
sources.paper: Serverless Cold Starts and Where to Find Them (EuroSys'25; arXiv 2410.06145)
sources.exact_schema: cold-start events: day, time(s), clusterName(int), funcName(int), userID(int), requestID(hash), totalCost_cold_start(s), podAllocationCost(s), deployCodeCost(s), deployDependencyCost(s), schedulingCost(s), podID(pool name encoding cpu/mem limits). trigger table: funcID, cpu_request(millicores), runtime, triggerType-invocationType.
sources.license: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC BY 4.0) — redistribution permitted WITH attribution. requestID/podID are author-hashed; NO raw prompt/response text. Commit policy: bounded derived numeric sample + attribution; requestID hash omitted from committed sample.
sources.classification: measured (FaaS cold-start) — but cold-start EVENT files exceed the 300MB cap
sources.measured_vs_derived: MEASURED FaaS cold-start cost breakdown (pod allocation, code deploy, dependency deploy, scheduling). This is CPU serverless pod startup, NOT GPU model-load. For GPU LLM usage it is a STRUCTURAL prior/proxy only, calibration-only, never server-class model_load_duration truth.
sources.bounded_ingest_method: Smallest cold-start region file R1.zip=467MB > 300MB cap and is a non-range-able zip; quantiles zips are 0.9-5.5GB. Used a bounded 100MB range-download of R1.zip + raw-DEFLATE-inflate of the first fully-contained member (R1/day_28.csv) -> 239,405 complete cold-start rows recovered (truncated tail discarded). Full 467MB+ download was NOT performed (would violate the bound). Trigger/runtime CSV (117KB) ingested in full.

downloaded.note: bounded 100MB slice of one region only

sources.gpu_coldstart_warning: Do NOT convert FaaS cold-start into GPU LLM cold-start. deployCodeCost/deployDependencyCost are code/dependency download, NOT model-weight load. Usable ONLY as a simulator-prior calibration of cold-start COST STRUCTURE.

sources.name: alibaba_gpu_v2025
sources.main_question: Can this add useful autoscaling or queue-risk proxy labels to the Economic ML Alpha audit?
sources.paper: GPU-Disaggregated Serving for Deep Learning Recommendation Models at Scale (NSDI'25)
sources.exact_schema: instance lifecycle records: instance_sn, role(CN/HN), app_name, cpu_request/limit, gpu_request/limit, rdma_request/limit, memory_request/limit, disk_request/limit, max_instance_per_node, creation_time(s), scheduled_time(s), deletion_time(s).
sources.license: Alibaba clusterdata repo terms (citation required; no standalone LICENSE at repo root - HTTP 404). Values are integers + hashed app/instance names; NO raw text. Commit policy: bounded derived numeric sample + citation.
sources.measured_vs_derived: MEASURED instance creation/scheduled/deletion timestamps + resource ALLOCATIONS (gpu_request). NOT per-request. NO measured serving queue_wait (only a DERIVABLE scheduler_delay = scheduled_time - creation_time). NO utilization (only allocation). NO failure/timeout. NO autoscaling EVENT labels (scaling can only be INFERRED from per-app create/delete counts over time).
sources.autoscaling_warning: Do NOT convert this proxy into measured serving autoscaling. There are no scale-up/scale-down event labels and no per-request queue. Any autoscaling/queue signal is an INFERRED instance-lifecycle proxy.

honest_constraints: Mooncake has NO measured reuse label -> derived prefix-reuse proxy only; label NOT identical to SwissAI reuse_percentage.
Huawei is FaaS (CPU pod) cold-start, NOT GPU model-load -> calibration-only prior; cold-start event files exceed 300MB cap so only a bounded 100MB slice of one region was recovered.
Alibaba v2025 is instance-lifecycle, NOT per-request -> autoscaling/queue are inferred proxies, no measured serving autoscaling or queue-wait.
